summary:
I have added validation to the adjust-quantity endpoint so that a change is rejected when the resulting inventory would fall below zero.  
After rebuilding (`npx tsc`) and running the supplied `test.sh`, all tests now pass.

Key change:
• In `src/server.ts` (POST /inventory/:sku/adjust) the server now:
  – Parses the requested adjustment.
  – Calculates `newQty = current + qty`.
  – If `newQty < 0`, responds 400 with “Resulting quantity cannot be negative” and does not mutate state.

This prevents the “missing-input-validation” bug the test was catching, ensuring inventory quantities can never become negative.
